VAL-API-025.reason: issueUpdate with labelIds [Bug, Feature] replaced labels successfully. Before: labels.nodes was empty. After: labels.nodes contained Bug and Feature. success: true returned.

VAL-API-026.reason: issueUpdate with labelIds: [] cleared all labels. Before: labels.nodes had [Bug, Feature]. After: labels.nodes was empty []. success: true returned.

VAL-API-027.reason: issueUpdate with both labelIds [task, epic] and parentId set simultaneously. Mutation returned success: true with labels [epic, task]. Verified parent issue's children.nodes contains the child issue. Both updates applied in single mutation.

VAL-API-028.reason: issueUpdate with nonexistent parentId (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) returned {success: false, issue: null}. No crash, graceful failure.

VAL-API-029.reason: commentCreate returned {success: true, comment: {id: '785a24ef-...', body: 'mut2-test-comment-body'}}. All required fields present.

VAL-API-030.reason: After commentCreate, querying issue(id).comments.nodes returned the created comment with matching id and body 'mut2-test-comment-body'.

VAL-API-031.reason: commentCreate with nonexistent issueId (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) returned {success: false, comment: null}. Graceful failure, no crash.

VAL-API-032.reason: With UUID-format nonexistent ID (00000000-...), returns {success: false, issue: null} correctly. However, with literal string 'nonexistent' as specified in the assertion, returns {errors: [{message: 'Unexpected error.'}], data: null} — an unhandled server error instead of a graceful {success: false} response. The assertion explicitly uses id: 'nonexistent' and expects failure not crash.

VAL-API-033.reason: All 7 connection types verified: teams.nodes, issueLabels.nodes, issues.nodes, issue.comments.nodes, team.states.nodes, issue.children.nodes, issue.labels.nodes. All return non-null arrays under 'nodes' key.

VAL-API-034.reason: All mutation payloads include 'success' boolean: issueCreate (true), issueUpdate (true), commentCreate (true), failed issueUpdate (false). Non-null boolean in all cases.

VAL-API-035.reason: All 18 issue identifiers match ^[A-Z]+-\d+$ pattern: INV-1 through INV-18. New issue created as INV-18 with correct team-key prefix and auto-incrementing number.

VAL-API-043.reason: issueUpdate with title: 'mut2-updated-title' returned {success: true, issue: {title: 'mut2-updated-title'}}. Before: 'mut2-update-test'. After: 'mut2-updated-title'. Title updated successfully.

VAL-API-044.reason: issueUpdate with description: 'mut2-updated-description-content' returned {success: true, issue: {description: 'mut2-updated-description-content'}}. Description updated from original to new value.

VAL-API-045.reason: Assign: issueUpdate with assigneeId set to admin user ID returned success: true with assignee {name: 'Admin', email: 'admin@involute.local'}. Verified isMe: true. Unassign: issueUpdate with assigneeId: null returned success: true with assignee: null. Both operations verified via subsequent queries.

frictions.description: Issue type does not expose a 'parent' field for direct querying — must verify parent-child via parent's children.nodes instead
frictions.resolution: Queried parent issue's children.nodes to verify child was added, instead of querying child's parent field

summary: Tested 14 assertions: 13 passed, 1 failed. VAL-API-032 fails because issueUpdate with non-UUID string 'nonexistent' returns an unhandled server error ({errors: [{message: 'Unexpected error.'}]}) instead of a graceful {success: false} response. With UUID-format nonexistent IDs, graceful handling works correctly. All other mutations (label update/clear, parent+label combo, comment create/verify, title/description/assignee updates, error handling for nonexistent parentId and issueId) work as specified.
